data.title: Infleqtion: Monetizing Quantum Sensing Ahead of a $160B Market Opportunity, Backed by Nvidia Partnership and Attractive Valuation
data.description:
Atif Malik, an analyst from Citi, has initiated a new Buy rating on Infleqtion (INFQ).

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Atif Malik has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, starting with Infleqtion’s ability to monetize quantum sensing while the broader quantum computing market is still maturing. He highlights that the firm’s neutral atom technology underpins both its sensing and computing products, enabling a full‑stack offering where precision sensors, such as atomic clocks, currently drive around two‑thirds of revenue and quantum computers contribute the rest.

He also underscores the company’s view of a very large long‑term market, with a total opportunity exceeding $160B by 2040 across computing and sensing applications like timing, navigation, gravity measurement, and RF detection. In addition, Malik points to Infleqtion’s partnership with Nvidia as strong external validation of its technology and software relevance for AI and future quantum workloads, and he considers the stock’s valuation attractive versus peers, supporting a $20 price target despite acknowledged technology, demand‑timing, and competitive risks.

In another report released on April 10, BTIG also initiated coverage with a Buy rating on the stock with a $22.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
